{"society_power": 6.0, "state_power": 5.0, "society_change": 0.5, "state_change": 0.5, "key_event": "Chehabist state-building amid 1958 crisis and Nasserist mobilization", "reasoning": "Chehab's administrative reforms (Banque du Liban, social security, extending reach to the periphery) modestly strengthened a still-weak state, though the 1958 war and reliance on U.S. intervention exposed its fragility, keeping it at the lower-moderate range. Meanwhile pan-Arab/Nasserist currents, the growing \"belt of misery,\" and energized sectarian constituencies deepened associational mobilization, nudging society power into solid moderate territory."}
